name: American Skullcap
summary: American skullcap is an aerial-parts monograph used primarily for calm, tension, and sleep-adjacent positioning. It is stack-relevant for gentle GABAergic support, but product identity matters because American and Chinese skullcap are not interchangeable.
description: American skullcap is used in teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts. Reported flavones include baicalin, baicalein, and scutellarin. Position as calming and nervous-system support rather than as a generic 'sedative' shortcut, and keep species identity explicit in frontend labels.
mechanism: Proposed mechanism centers on flavone-driven GABAergic and neuroinhibitory support with additional anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
safetyNotes: Usually tolerated, but drowsiness, dizziness, or additive sedation are possible in sensitive users or higher-dose combinations.
dosage: Product-specific extract dosing; traditional tea/tincture use common
preparation: Teas, tinctures, capsules, and aerial-parts extracts.
region: Native to North America; especially eastern and central United States and Canada.
